title: German Chancellor Merz hints at possible cabinet reshuffle after top CDU figure resigns
content: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has hinted he could reshuffle his government after Jens Spahn resigned as parliamentary leader, adding to pressure on the ruling conservatives ahead of key regional elections.

title: Thousands of Italian military and police personnel march in Rome over pay and pensions
content: Thousands of members of the security and defence forces demonstrated on Saturday, urging the government to tackle the economic crisis and guarantee greater protection for workers engaged in defending the State.

title: Two major wildfires rage in Spain as burned area nears 50,000 hectares
content: The La Mierla wildfire has scorched nearly 9,900 hectares and forced the evacuation of 16 villages in central Spain. Another 15,400 hectares have burned near Orés in northeastern Spain, bringing the country's wildfire toll to around 50,000 hectares this year.

title: At least one killed as Russia launches overnight missile and drone attack on Kyiv
content: Russia launched 41 missiles and 125 drones at Ukraine overnight, a day after Kyiv sent drones across the border into Russia.
snippet: Russia launched 41 missiles and 125 drones at Ukraine overnight, a day after Kyiv sent drones across the border into Russia.

title: Goose flock behind crash of Siemens CEO's family in New York
content: For the Siemens CEO from Barcelona and his family of five, it was meant to be an unforgettable helicopter tour over New York. Instead, the flight ended in tragedy: the aircraft crashed into the Hudson River. Investigators now know why.

title: Spain praises public education but slashes nearly €309m from its funding for Presidency staff pay
content: Cabinet approves moving €309.8 million from Education to the Presidency, Justice and Parliamentary Relations ministry to cover staff costs under an extended state budget.
snippet: Cabinet approves moving €309.8 million from Education to the Presidency, Justice and Parliamentary Relations ministry to cover staff costs under an extended state budget.

title: Wildfires in Spain: Zaragoza blaze destroys over 15,400 hectares
content: Two large wildfires in Zaragoza and Guadalajara are keeping Spain on edge. Neither fire is under control, and hundreds remain evacuated as crews battle the blaze.
snippet: Two large wildfires in Zaragoza and Guadalajara are keeping Spain on edge. Neither fire is under control, and hundreds remain evacuated as crews battle the blaze.

title: Deaths from Venezuela earthquakes rise above 5,000
content: Venezuela has been able to unlock €302 million of funding from the International Monetary Fund to support recovery and reconstruction following the devastating earthquakes that hit the northern coastal region last month.

title: CDU's Jens Spahn resigns amid growing scrutiny over surrogate baby
content: Following Spahn's resignation, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz described the move as "the right one" and "unavoidable." "Credibility is the highest asset in politics," the chancellor added.
snippet: Following Spahn's resignation, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz described the move as "the right one" and "unavoidable." "Credibility is the highest asset in politics," the chancellor added.

title: 'Make America Read Again': Venice protesters greet US ambassador with protest signs
content: US Ambassador to Italy arrived in Venice aboard the 117-meter luxury yacht Boardwalk, which features two helipads, two swimming pools, and a fully equipped spa and gym.

title: Latvia becomes Lukashenka's latest pressure point for the EU
content: Belarus has shifted migrant routes towards Latvia's border, forcing Riga to call on its neighbours for support as the Baltic state heads into an election campaign. Lithuania is now considering whether to introduce temporary border controls to manage a rise in secondary migration from Latvia.

title: Macron and Merz agree to deepen defence ties and push for tougher stance on China
content: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz welcomed French President Emmanuel Macron to the Noervenich air base near Cologne on Friday for the 26th Franco-German ministerial council.
snippet: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz welcomed French President Emmanuel Macron to the Noervenich air base near Cologne on Friday for the 26th Franco-German ministerial council.

title: Seven people die in Russian missile and drone attacks on Ukraine
content: A number regions of Ukraine have been hit by Russian drones and missiles over the past day. Overall seven people are reported to have died, and at least 67 injured across the country.
snippet: A number regions of Ukraine have been hit by Russian drones and missiles over the past day. Overall seven people are reported to have died, and at least 67 injured across the country.

title: Rule of Law in Hungary shows ‘radical change’ under Magyar, EU says
content: The latest European Commission report highlights “intense reform efforts” by Budapest's new government. The decision to join the European Public Prosecutor’s Office is seen as a key step in the fight against corruption.

title: Italian police conduct raids on several luxury brands in labour abuse probe
content: At issue is the near-ubiquitous practice of brands subcontracting work to suppliers that in turn contract to others, ending up with firms who employ Chinese workers in exploitative conditions.
snippet: At issue is the near-ubiquitous practice of brands subcontracting work to suppliers that in turn contract to others, ending up with firms who employ Chinese workers in exploitative conditions.

title: Supporters welcome France's assisted dying law
content: Supporters welcomed France's assisted dying law after parliament approved the landmark legislation, calling it "a huge relief" for people with incurable illnesses. The law still requires approval from France's Constitutional Council.

title: EU orders Google to share search data, open Android to AI rivals competitors
content: Brussels said Google must start sharing search data from January 2027 and users would start benefitting from the changes to Android from July 2027.
snippet: Brussels said Google must start sharing search data from January 2027 and users would start benefitting from the changes to Android from July 2027.

title: Ex-CEO of Italian motorway operator among 32 convicted over Genoa bridge collapse
content: Dozens of family members of the victims packed the courtroom as Chief Judge Paolo Lepri read the verdicts against 57 defendants. Many relatives broke down in tears as the sentences were read.
snippet: Dozens of family members of the victims packed the courtroom as Chief Judge Paolo Lepri read the verdicts against 57 defendants. Many relatives broke down in tears as the sentences were read.

title: Storm-1516: Fake Hezbollah video threatening Bastille Day attack possibly Russia linked
content: A social media video emerged ahead of this year's Bastille Day in which alleged Hezbollah militants threatened attacks on French soil. The video turned out to be fake, and researchers say it could be linked to a well-known Russian disinformation campaign.

title: UK by-election: Nigel Farage faces 'record' number of rivals
content: A fresh by-election has been called in the UK constituency of Clacton, after Nigel Farage, the leader of the right-wing Reform UK party, resigned his seat to seek re-election. Farage had won the seat in 2024, securing 46% of the vote share.

title: Deschamps' last match, golden boot race: what is at stake in third-place play-off
content: France take on England in Miami on Saturday at 11 p.m. local time, in the third-place play-off match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup finals.

title: Why the EU’s so-called Chat Control law has privacy experts up in arms
content: The European Parliament has approved extraordinary measures to fight child sex abuse online. The rules have been dubbed "Chat Control" by critics, who warn of mass surveillance. Supporters reject these claims and argue a balance between protecting children and privacy rights is possible.

title: Polish President Karol Nawrocki vetoes civil partnerships bill
content: The president's veto can be overridden, but it would take a three-fifths majority vote in the lower house of parliament, a rarity in Poland.
snippet: The president's veto can be overridden, but it would take a three-fifths majority vote in the lower house of parliament, a rarity in Poland.

title: Saving Europe’s farmland: Agri-PV leads the way towards sustainable solar panel use
content: The availability of cheap solar panels has led to a massive expansion of photovoltaic systems in rural areas. But will this result in a loss of farmland? It doesn’t have to be that way: a team of German inventors has developed systems that allow farmers to reap a double harvest: crops & solar energy

title: Brussels to double electrification target in bid to cut fossil fuel consumption
content: After months of speculation, the European Commission announced a 46 percent electrification target for the bloc's economy by 2040. The move is intended to cut dependencies on imported fossil fuels but critics argue that high electricity prices may complicate the EU's goals.

title: 'Shameless': Greece and LNG emerge as major roadblock in new Russia sanctions
content: Greece has thrown a spanner into the works of fresh EU sanctions by demanding an exemption to a full ban on Russian LNG. Member states are aghast at the request, with one diplomat calling it "shameless".

title: Hungary's Magyar demands migration fine refund from EU
content: The Hungarian leader argues that the EU's stance has shifted as other member states pursue similar border policies. He also wants €2 billion in lost recovery funds returned in an upcoming budget deal.

title: International flights set to pay carbon costs under EU's carbon market review from 2029
content: Under the European Union's revised carbon market rules, international flights arriving in Europe from destinations within 5,000 km will be required to pay for their CO2 emissions starting in 2029.
snippet: Under the European Union's revised carbon market rules, international flights arriving in Europe from destinations within 5,000 km will be required to pay for their CO2 emissions starting in 2029.

title: Spain's largest wildfire of the year burns 12,000 hectares in Zaragoza
content: The Cinco Villas wildfire in Zaragoza has burned 12,000 hectares and forced the evacuation of six villages. Guadalajara and Madrid are battling two other wildfires, with more than 2,000 people ordered to remain indoors.

title: How do citizens of NATO countries view the alliance?
content: NATO has often been in the headlines in recent years, amid Russia's war in Ukraine and US President Donald Trump's threats against fellow member states and other crises. title: Spain 'strongest' team, EU Sport Commissioner backs La Roja
content: Glenn Micallef, EU sport commissioner, says the expanded World Cup format "worked" ahead of Sunday's final. Micallef says the tournament reflects the improvement of football worldwide, but insists the "best talent and best football is still being played in Europe", making him "proud".

title: A Greek tragedy, and a postcard from Djibouti

title: Watch: massive hailstones smash homes and cars during violent storms in France
content: Violent storms brought giant hailstones, heavy rain and powerful winds to several parts of France. Videos show large hail battering homes and cars. The storms turned deadly, with two people killed in weather-related incidents as much of the country remained under weather alerts.

title: Ukrainian parliament backs new wartime cabinet, amid scandal over defence minister dismissal
